Arguments: {"filePath":"d:\\Ai2050\\TransformerLens-Project\\tests\\glm5\\ccml_phase33_critical_verification.py","old_str":"        np.random.seed(42)\n        rand_dir = np.random.randn(d_model)\n        rand_dir = rand_dir / (np.linalg.norm(rand_dir) + 1e-10)\n        \n        for concept, grad in gradient_directions.items():\n            grad_norm = grad / (np.linalg.norm(grad) + 1e-10)\n            \n            cos_probe = compute_cos(grad_norm, edible_probe)\n            cos_animacy = compute_cos(grad_norm, animacy_probe)\n            cos_random = compute_cos(grad_norm, rand_dir)\n            \n            grad_probe_coss[concept] = float(cos_probe)\n            grad_random_coss[concept] = float(cos_random)\n            \n            print(f\"      {concept}: grad·probe_edible={cos_probe:.4f}, \"\n                  f\"grad·probe_animacy={cos_animacy:.4f}, grad·random={cos_random:.4f}\")\n        \n        # ===== 因果效力测试 =====\n        # 沿梯度方向、probe方向、随机方向扰动，看对edible logit的影响\n        \n        print(f\"\\n    ★★★ 因果效力测试: 沿不同方向扰动对edible logit的影响 ★★★\")\n        \n        eps_values = [0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0]\n        \n        causal_test = {}\n        \n        for concept in [\"apple\", \"dog\", \"hammer\"]:\n            if concept not in valid_words or concept not in gradient_directions:\n                continue\n            \n            c_idx = valid_words.index(concept)\n            h_orig = H[c_idx]\n            \n            grad_dir = gradient_directions[concept] / (np.linalg.norm(gradient_directions[concept]) + 1e-10)\n            \n            # 方向列表\n            directions = {\n                \"gradient\": grad_dir,\n                \"probe_edible\": edible_probe,\n                \"probe_animacy\": animacy_probe,\n                \"random\": rand_dir,\n                \"random2\": np.random.randn(d_model) / (np.linalg.norm(np.random.randn(d_model)) + 1e-10),\n            }\n            \n            concept_causal = {}\n            \n            for dir_name, dir_vec in directions.items():\n                logit_changes = []\n                \n                for eps in eps_values:\n                    h_pert = h_orig + eps * dir_vec * np.linalg.norm(h_orig - H_mean[0])\n                    \n                    h_tensor = torch.tensor(h_pert, dtype=model.lm_head.weight.dtype, \n                                           device=device).unsqueeze(0)\n                    with torch.no_grad():\n                        logits_pert = model.lm_head(h_tensor)\n                    \n                    # edible logit变化\n                    if len(edible_token_ids) > 0:\n                        edible_logit_pert = float(logits_pert[0, edible_token_ids].mean())\n                    else:\n                        edible_logit_pert = 0\n                    \n                    h_tensor_orig = torch.tensor(h_orig, dtype=model.lm_head.weight.dtype, \n                                                device=device).unsqueeze(0)\n                    with torch.no_grad():\n                        logits_orig = model.lm_head(h_tensor_orig)\n                    \n                    if len(edible_token_ids) > 0:\n                        edible_logit_orig = float(logits_orig[0, edible_token_ids].mean())\n                    else:\n                        edible_logit_orig = 0\n                    \n                    delta = edible_logit_pert - edible_logit_orig\n                    logit_changes.append(float(delta))\n                \n                concept_causal[dir_name] = {\n                    \"eps_values\": eps_values,\n                    \"edible_logit_changes\": logit_changes,\n                }","new_str":"        np.random.seed(42)\n        rand_dir = np.random.randn(d_model)\n        rand_dir = rand_dir / (np.linalg.norm(rand_dir) + 1e-10)\n        \n        # 梯度投影比较 (数值梯度版本)\n        print(f\"\\n    ★★★ 梯度投影 vs Probe方向 (数值有限差分) ★★★\")\n        for concept, grad_projs in gradient_directions.items():\n            probe_ed = grad_projs.get(\"probe_edible\", 0)\n            probe_an = grad_projs.get(\"probe_animacy\", 0)\n            rand_proj = grad_projs.get(\"random\", 0)\n            print(f\"      {concept}: ∂logit/∂(probe_edible)={probe_ed:.4f}, \"\n                  f\"∂logit/∂(probe_animacy)={probe_an:.4f}, ∂logit/∂(random)={rand_proj:.4f}\")\n        \n        # ===== 因果效力测试 =====\n        print(f\"\\n    ★★★ 因果效力测试: 沿不同方向扰动对edible logit的影响 ★★★\")\n        \n        eps_values = [0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0]\n        \n        causal_test = {}\n        \n        for concept in [\"apple\", \"dog\", \"hammer\"]:\n            if concept not in valid_words:\n                continue\n            \n            c_idx = valid_words.index(concept)\n            h_orig = H[c_idx]\n            \n            # 方向列表 (没有autograd梯度, 只用probe和随机)\n            directions = {\n                \"probe_edible\": edible_probe,\n                \"probe_animacy\": animacy_probe,\n                \"random\": rand_dir,\n                \"random2\": np.random.randn(d_model) / (np.linalg.norm(np.random.randn(d_model)) + 1e-10),\n            }\n            \n            # 先计算原始logit\n            h_tensor_orig = torch.tensor(h_orig, dtype=model.lm_head.weight.dtype, \n                                        device=device).unsqueeze(0)\n            with torch.no_grad():\n                logits_orig = model.lm_head(h_tensor_orig)\n            if len(edible_token_ids) > 0:\n                edible_logit_orig = float(logits_orig[0, edible_token_ids].mean())\n            else:\n                edible_logit_orig = 0\n            \n            concept_causal = {}\n            \n            for dir_name, dir_vec in directions.items():\n                logit_changes = []\n                \n                for eps in eps_values:\n                    h_pert = h_orig + eps * dir_vec * np.linalg.norm(h_orig - H_mean[0])\n                    \n                    h_tensor = torch.tensor(h_pert, dtype=model.lm_head.weight.dtype, \n                                           device=device).unsqueeze(0)\n                    with torch.no_grad():\n                        logits_pert = model.lm_head(h_tensor)\n                    \n                    if len(edible_token_ids) > 0:\n                        edible_logit_pert = float(logits_pert[0, edible_token_ids].mean())\n                    else:\n                        edible_logit_pert = 0\n                    \n                    delta = edible_logit_pert - edible_logit_orig\n                    logit_changes.append(float(delta))\n                \n                concept_causal[dir_name] = {\n                    \"eps_values\": eps_values,\n                    \"edible_logit_changes\": logit_changes,\n                }"}

{"type":"reasoning","text":"Now I have all the data. Let me compile the Phase 33 MEMO update. The key findings are:\n\n**ExpA: Layer Transformation Linearity**\n- Qwen3: L0→L9 R²=0.987 (residual encodes attributes R²=0.58-0.74), L9→L18 R²≈0.99999, L18→L27 R²≈0.99999\n- GLM4: L0→L10 R²=0.768 (residual encodes attributes R²=0.86-0.88!), L10→L20 R²=0.982, L20→L30 R²=0.998\n- DS7B: L0→L7 R²=0.9999, L7→L14 R²≈0.9999999, L14→L21 R²≈0.99999997\n- A^{-T}w_L1 ≈ w_L2 is REJECTED (cos≈0.001-0.02) for all models!\n- Condition number of A is ~10^8-10^11 (extremely ill-conditioned)\n\nKey insight: The transformation is nearly linear (R²>0.999 for deep layers), but A^{-T}w_L1≠w_L2 because the transformation matrix A is extremely ill-conditioned (condition number ~10^10). The null space of A is huge, so the pseudoinverse is essentially random in those directions.\n\nGLM4 is special: L0→L10 R²=0.768 with residual encoding attributes (R²=0.86-0.88), but adding quadratic terms doesn't improve R². This means the transformation between L0 and L10 is poorly captured by linear AND quadratic models.\n\n**ExpB: Intrinsic Dimension**\n- Local PCA rank: 8-12 across all layers and models\n- MLE dimension (k=15): 5-14 depending on layer\n- Global PCA 95%: 83-179 dimensions\n- Random baseline MLE: 27-35 (much higher than real data)\n- Two-NN: median ~7-19 (much lower than mean, indicating heavy-tailed distribution)\n\nKey insight: Local intrinsic dimension ≈ 8-12, global PCA 95% ≈ 100-180. The gap between local and global dimension is a signature of a curved manifold.\n\n**ExpC: Gradient Attribution (Causal Direction)**\n- Qwen3 (autograd): Gradient direction cos≈0.02 with probe (nearly orthogonal!), but gradient has 4-75x more causal effect than probe_edible\n  - L27: gradient Δlogit=21.2 vs probe_edible=-0.28 for apple (75x difference!)\n  - L18: probe_edible DECREASES edible logit (negative!) while gradient increases it\n  - L9: gradient ≈ 2x more effective than probe_edible\n- GLM4 (numerical): L30 probe_edible DECREASES edible logit (-0.26), probe_animacy INCREASES it (+0.56)!\n  - L0: all directions have tiny effects (~0.001)\n- DS7B (numerical): L21 probe_edible DECREASES edible logit (-0.25 to -0.375), random direction INCREASES it (+0.125 to +0.187)\n\nKEY FINDING: probe_edible direction actually DECREASES edible logit in deep layers! This means:\n1. Probe direction ≠ causal direction (confirmed with extreme prejudice)\n2. Probe reads statistical correlation, not causal mechanism\n3. The true causal direction for \"edible\" is nearly orthogonal to the probe direction\n\nNow let me write the MEMO update."}

- 这个项目的目标是实现agi，核心思路：深度神经网络提取了大脑中的某种数学结构，产生了语言能力，语言能力包括了语义，逻辑，语法等维度，有完整规则，说明背后有某种数学结构，这个数学结构很可能超过了现有认知，所以不要轻易使用任何统计学方法和高级数学理论，只进行基础分析，当完成大量拼图后，让结论自然体现出来.

如果需要进行模型测试，请基于cuda使用本地硬盘上的qwen3，GLM4 8bit和DS7B 8bit完成测试，注意测试完一个再测试另外一个，避免gpu内存溢出。
设计测试方案时，不能样本太少导致判断错误，也不能太大导致时间太长。而且要避免出现k为固定值，得到ratio=2.2，把特殊情况当初普遍结论的情况。

<analysis>
The conversation spans Phase 31-33 of a mechanistic interpretability research project on LLMs (Qwen3, GLM4, DeepSeek7B). The user provides two major critical reviews that correct the agent's overconfident conclusions. Phase 31 identified subspace issues; Phase 32 explored manifold geometry but overclaimed "attribute=function g(h)"; Phase 33 ran critical verification experiments (layer-wise linearity, intrinsic dimension, gradient attribution) that substantially revised the framework. Key files: MEMO at `d:\Ai2050\TransformerLens-Project\research\glm5\docs\AGI_GLM5_MEMO.md`, scripts in `tests/glm5/`, results in `tests/glm5_temp/`. Multiple bugs were fixed (PCA inverse_transform shape, OOM on GLM4 gradient computation, variable scoping issues).
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user is guiding a mechanistic interpretability research project investigating how LLMs encode semantic attributes (edible, animacy, size). The user provides two detailed critical reviews (after Phase 31 and Phase 32) identifying logical fallacies, overconfident conclusions, and methodological flaws. Each time, the user asks the agent to: (a) analyze whether the criticism is correct, (b) adopt valid corrections, and (c) continue the research with better-designed experiments. The overarching goal is to discover the true mathematical structure of semantic encoding in transformer models.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Subspace vs Manifold**: Probing whether semantic attributes live in linear subspaces or nonlinear manifolds
   - **Probe ≠ Causal**: Linear probes find statistical correlations, NOT causal directions (decisively proven in Phase 33)
   - **Layer-wise transformations**: Mid-to-deep layers are nearly linear (R²>0.999); shallow-to-mid layers are significantly nonlinear (R²=0.77-0.99)
   - **Ill-conditioned matrices**: Inter-layer transform A has condition number ~10⁸-10¹¹, huge null space explains cross-layer probe weight differences without needing nonlinear functions
   - **Intrinsic dimension**: Local MLE dim ≈ 8-12, global PCA 95% ≈ 100-180 → signature of curved manifold
   - **Gradient attribution**: True causal gradient directions are nearly orthogonal to probe directions (cos≈0.02); gradient is 75-96x more causally effective than probe direction
   - **Redundant/distributed encoding**: Deleting a subspace doesn't delete information; attributes exist across subspace + orthogonal space
   - **Tangent space interpretation**: Subspaces are local linear approximations (tangent spaces), not the manifold itself

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`d:\Ai2050\TransformerLens-Project\research\glm5\docs\AGI_GLM5_MEMO.md`**: Main research memo, updated with Phase 32 and Phase 33 results. Contains the evolving theoretical framework and all experimental findings.
   - **`d:\Ai2050\TransformerLens-Project\tests\glm5\ccml_phase32_manifold_geometry.py`**: Phase 32 script with 3 experiments: (A) scale-dependent linearity via KL divergence, (B) cross-layer probe consistency, (C) autoencoder interpolation. Key finding: probe weights differ (cos≈0.5) but predictions agree (r>0.999).
   - **`d:\Ai2050\TransformerLens-Project\tests\glm5\ccml_phase33_critical_verification.py`**: Phase 33 script with 3 experiments: (A) layer-wise linearity (fit h_L2 = A·h_L1), (B) intrinsic dimension (MLE/Two-NN/local PCA), (C) gradient attribution vs probe directions. This script was heavily modified to fix OOM and variable scoping bugs.
   - **Result files**: `tests/glm5_temp/ccml_exp{A-C}_{qwen3|glm4|deepseek7b}_results.json` for both Phase 32 and 33.

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **PCA inverse_transform shape error**: `h_pca_interp` was 1D but `inverse_transform` needed 2D → fixed with `.reshape(1, -1)`
   - **GLM4 OOM during autograd**: 8-bit quantized model can't do autograd → switched to numerical gradient (finite differences) using lm_head directly
   - **Variable scoping**: `edible_probe` and `animacy_probe` were used before definition in the gradient section → moved definitions before the loop
   - **gradient_directions structure change**: Changed from `{concept: vector}` to `{concept: {dir_name: projection}}` for numerical gradient approach, required updating downstream analysis code
   - **User criticism corrections**: (1) "Delete subspace no effect → subspace unimportant" was wrong → should be "redundant encoding"; (2) "Orthogonal intervention works → subspace fails" was wrong → should be "distributed non-orthogonal encoding"; (3) "Interpolation fails → subspace invalid" was wrong → should be "subspace = tangent space"; (4) "Lasso=3-5 dims = true dimension" was overclaim → Lasso only captures strongest linearly readable part; (5) "Cross-layer consistency proves nonlinear function g(h)" was logical leap → could be explained by linear transform with ill-conditioned matrix

5. Problem Solving:
   - Resolved the Phase 31 overclaim that "subspaces are unimportant" by recognizing redundant encoding
   - Resolved the Phase 32 overclaim that "attribute = nonlinear function g(h)" by showing cross-layer consistency can be partially explained by linear transforms with huge null spaces
   - Discovered the most striking finding: **probe directions are NOT causal** — moving along probe_edible direction actually DECREASES edible logit in deep layers, while gradient directions are 75-96x more effective
   - Established that intrinsic dimension ≈ 8-12 locally vs 100-180 globally, supporting curved manifold hypothesis

6. All User Messages:
   - **Message 1** (after Phase 31): Detailed critique identifying 4 "hard flaws" in Phase 31 conclusions: (1) deleting subspace ≠ deleting information (redundant encoding), (2) orthogonal space containing info ≠ subspace failure (non-orthogonal encoding), (3) interpolation failure ≠ subspace invalid (tangent space vs manifold), (4) Lasso dims ≠ true dimension. Proposed "attribute = function g(h)" on a manifold. Asked agent to analyze correctness and continue research.
   - **Message 2** (after Phase 32): Detailed critique identifying logical leaps: (1) cross-layer consistency ≠ proof of nonlinear function (could be linear transform h_L2=A·h_L1), (2) KL perturbation doesn't prove causality, (3) haven't truly measured "manifold" (no intrinsic dim/geodesic/curvature), (4) Lasso overcorrection, (5) AE interpolation "leaf" is weak evidence. Proposed 3 critical next experiments: verify layer-wise linearity, measure intrinsic dimension, find true causal directions via gradient. Asked agent to analyze correctness and continue.
   - **Message 3**: "继续完成memo文件的更新，并报告进展" (Continue completing the memo file update and report progress)
   - **Message 4**: "继续完成memo文件的更新" (Continue completing the memo file update)

   The Phase 33 MEMO update was completed with all experimental results and revised theoretical framework. The key paradigm shift is from "attribute = direction" → "attribute = distributed causal structure with probe≠causal, linear transforms with null space effects, and locally low-dimensional but globally curved geometry."
</summary>

<user_query>
关键问题（有明显逻辑漏洞的地方）
❗问题1：gradient = 因果方向 → 这个推断不成立（非常关键）

你现在的核心跳跃是：

梯度方向 ≈ 因果方向

但实际：

❌ 梯度只是“局部最陡上升方向”
∇
h
	

ℓ

它表示：

在当前点
对当前 logit
的一阶变化

但它不是：

全局控制方向
稳定语义方向
可解释特征方向
🚨 举个关键问题：

梯度是 input-dependent 的：

apple 的 gradient ≠ dog 的 gradient
同一 token 在不同上下文 gradient 也不同

👉 所以你现在实际上发现的是：

“模型的决策方向是高度依赖输入的”

这叫：

local decision boundary geometry

不是：

“存在一个真实语义方向”

✔ 正确说法应该是：

gradient 是局部因果敏感方向，不是语义方向

❗问题2：probe 方向负效应 ≠ “完全无用”

你现在的结论：

probe 是假的方向

这个太强了。

更准确是：

✔ probe 捕捉的是：
E[y∣h]

而不是：

dh
dy
	

这两者区别是核心：
类型	含义
probe	静态相关性
gradient	局部变化率

👉 在非线性系统中，这两个可以完全不一致

甚至：

correlation 正
derivative 负

👉 这是完全可能的（非单调函数）

❗问题3：关于“病态矩阵解释一切”——过度解释

你说：

A condition number 很大 → 零空间解释 probe 差异

这里有两个问题：

⚠️ (1) 高 condition number 在高维很常见

d_model ~ 4000：

几乎所有 learned matrix 都会 condition number 很大
这不是特殊现象

👉 所以不能直接用它解释结构

⚠️ (2) A⁻ᵀw ≠ w 的原因不止零空间

还包括：

非正交基
多解性（overparameterized）
噪声拟合
数据分布变化

👉 你现在把“零空间”作为唯一解释 → 过拟合解释

❗问题4：残差 = 非线性语义载体 → 证据不够

你看到：

residual 有高 R²

但问题是：

❌ residual ≠ 非线性语义

它可能是：

未被线性模型拟合的线性结构
噪声 + bias
局部曲率

👉 你没有证明：

r=f
nonlinear
	

(h)

只是证明：

linearmodel不够
❗问题5：内在维度 ≠ 流形（经典误区）

你现在推：

局部维度低 → 流形

但实际上：

❌ 还可能是：
clustered high-dim cloud
mixture of subspaces
anisotropic Gaussian

👉 真正流形需要：

光滑性（smoothness）
可微结构
geodesic consistency

你现在都没测

🚧 三、真正的瓶颈（最关键）
🧱 瓶颈1：你在混淆三个层级

这是目前最大的结构问题：

1️⃣ 表示空间（representation）
h 是什么结构
2️⃣ 解码空间（readout）
probe / W_U
3️⃣ 因果路径（computation）
h → h → logits

👉 你现在把：

probe（读出）
gradient（最后一层）
表示结构

混在一起解释

🧱 瓶颈2：你没有建模“路径”，只看端点

你现在做的是：

h
L
	

→logits

但真实机制是：

h
L
	

→h
L+1
	

→...→logits

👉 关键缺失：

中间层 Jacobian chain
信息如何逐层流动
🧱 瓶颈3：你还在“找坐标”，但系统是“算子”

你已经接近正确方向了：

模型不是在存“属性坐标”，而是在执行变换

但还没完全跳出来。

🧠 四、你现在最接近的真实结构

我帮你把目前最靠谱的版本写出来：

✔ 更合理的模型（Phase 33修正版）
1. 表示空间:
   h ∈ R^d 是高度冗余、分布式编码

2. 层间:
   h_{l+1} ≈ A_l h_l + 非线性残差
   - 中深层: A_l 主导
   - 浅层: 非线性主导

3. 属性:
   不是方向，而是：
   g(h) = 复杂非线性函数

4. probe:
   学的是 E[g(h) | h] 的线性近似

5. gradient:
   ∇h logit = 局部决策边界方向
   ≠ 全局语义结构

6. 因果:
   是路径上的 Jacobian chain
   不是单层方向
🚀 五、下一步真正该做什么（关键突破点）

你已经不该再：

❌ 找方向
❌ 找子空间
❌ 做线性probe

✅ 应该转向三个关键问题：
🔥 1. Jacobian chain（最关键）

测：

∂h
L
	

∂h
L+1
	

	


然后：

连乘 Jacobian
看信息如何传播

👉 这才是“因果路径”

🔥 2. feature attribution across layers

不是：

h → logit

而是：

哪些维度在每一层被放大/抑制
🔥 3. representation flow（流）

你要看的是：

表示如何在空间中“移动”

而不是：

它在哪个方向

🧾 最终总结（一句话）

👉 你现在最重要的突破是：

已经证明：语义不是方向，也不是子空间，而是一个通过层间变换实现的分布式计算过程

但当前最大问题是：

❗把“局部梯度”误当成“全局因果结构”，以及用线性工具解释本质非线性系统。                   分析以上内容是否正确，综合正确的部分和当前进展，继续完成任务

{"type":"reasoning","text":"The user is providing a detailed critical review of Phase 33's conclusions, identifying several logical flaws and suggesting next steps. Let me analyze each point carefully:\n\n1. **Gradient ≠ Causal direction**: The user is correct that gradient is input-dependent, local, and not a stable semantic direction. This is a valid criticism.\n\n2. **Probe direction negative effect ≠ completely useless**: Correct - probe captures E[y|h], gradient captures dh/dy, these are different in nonlinear systems.\n\n3. **Ill-conditioned matrix over-explanation**: Correct - high condition number is common in high dimensions and null space isn't the only explanation.\n\n4. **Residual ≠ nonlinear semantics**: Correct - residual could be linear structure not captured by the model, noise, bias, or local curvature.\n\n5. **Intrinsic dimension ≠ manifold**: Correct - low local dimension could also be clustered high-dim cloud, mixture of subspaces, anisotropic Gaussian.\n\nThe three bottlenecks identified:\n1. Confusing representation, readout, and computation\n2. Not modeling the path, only endpoints\n3. Still looking for coordinates when the system is operators\n\nThe user suggests the next steps should focus on:\n1. Jacobian chain - most critical\n2. Feature attribution across layers\n3. Second pass: inject perturbation at layer l, continue forward\n\nFor the second pass, I can use hooks:\n- Register a hook at layer l that adds δ to the output\n- Let the model run from input to output\n- The hook at layer l will modify the activation, and subsequent layers will see the modified version\n\nThis is standard activation patching. Let me implement it.\n\n```python\ndef perturbation_propagation(model, tokenizer, device, concept, template,\n                             layer_idx, direction, epsilon, edible_token_ids):\n    \"\"\"\n    Inject perturbation at layer_idx along direction, measure effect on output\n    \n    Returns:\n        delta_logit: change in edible logit\n        h_next_delta: change in next layer's hidden state (if captured)\n    \"\"\"\n    sent = template.format(word=concept)\n    toks = tokenizer(sent, return_tensors=\"pt\").to(device)\n    input_ids = toks.input_ids\n    tokens_list = [safe_decode(tokenizer, t) for t in input_ids[0].tolist()]\n    dep_idx = find_token_index(tokens_list, concept)\n    \n    if dep_idx < 0:\n        return None\n    \n    # First pass: get baseline h_l and logits\n    layers = get_layers(model)\n    target_layer = layers[layer_idx]\n    \n    captured_baseline = {}\n    def hook_baseline(module, input, output):\n        if isinstance(output, tuple):\n            captured_baseline['h'] = output[0].detach()\n        else:\n            captured_baseline['h'] = output.detach()\n    \n    handle = target_layer.register_forward_hook(hook_baseline)\n    with torch.no_grad():\n        outputs_baseline = model(**toks)\n        logits_baseline = outputs_baseline.logits\n    handle.remove()\n    \n    h_l_baseline = captured_baseline['h'][0, dep_idx, :]  # [d_model]\n    \n    if len(edible_token_ids) > 0:\n        edible_logit_baseline = float(logits_baseline[0, -1, edible_token_ids].mean())\n    else:\n        edible_logit_baseline = 0.0\n    \n    # Second pass: inject perturbation at layer_idx\n    captured_next = {}\n    next_layer_idx = min(layer_idx + 1, len(layers) - 1)\n    next_layer = layers[next_layer_idx]\n    \n    def hook_pert(module, input, output):\n        if isinstance(output, tuple):\n            out = output[0].clone()\n        else:\n            out = output.clone()\n        # Add perturbation at dep_idx position\n        out[0, dep_idx, :] += epsilon * torch.tensor(direction, \n            dtype=out.dtype, device=device)\n        if isinstance(output, tuple):\n            return (out,) + output[1:]\n        return out\n    \n    def hook_next(module, input, output):\n        if isinstance(output, tuple):\n            captured_next['h'] = output[0].detach()\n        else:\n            captured_next['h'] = output.detach()\n    \n    handle_pert = target_layer.register_forward_hook(hook_pert)\n    handle_next = next_layer.register_forward_hook(hook_next) if next_layer_idx != layer_idx else None\n    with torch.no_grad():\n        outputs_pert = model(**toks)\n        logits_pert = outputs_pert.logits\n    handle_pert.remove()\n    if handle_next:\n        handle_next.remove()\n    \n    if len(edible_token_ids) > 0:\n        edible_logit_pert = float(logits_pert[0, -1, edible_token_ids].mean())\n    else:\n        edible_logit_pert = 0.0\n    \n    delta_logit = edible_logit_pert - edible_logit_baseline\n    \n    # Also measure change in next layer's hidden state\n    if 'h' in captured_next:\n        h_next_pert = captured_next['h'][0, dep_idx, :].cpu().numpy()\n    else:\n        h_next_pert = None\n    \n    return {\n        'delta_logit': delta_logit,\n        'edible_logit_baseline': edible_logit_baseline,\n        'edible_logit_pert': edible_logit_pert,\n    }\n```\n\nWait, there's a problem. However, I'm only capturing the next layer's output — I need to also capture the baseline next layer output to compute the change.\n\nLet me restructure:\n\n1. First pass: collect h_l and h_{l+1} at baseline\n2. Manageable.\n\nBut wait, for efficiency, I can capture all layers' outputs in a single forward pass by registering hooks at all layers.\n\nLet me also think about what specific directions to test:\n1. **probe_edible**: the probe's direction for edible\n2. **probe_animacy**: the probe's direction for animacy\n3. **gradient_direction**: the numerical gradient direction (from Phase 33)\n4. **random**: random unit vector\n5. **PCA_top1**: top PCA direction of the hidden states at that layer\n\nActually, for the gradient direction, in Phase 33 we only computed it for Qwen3. For other models, we used numerical gradients (finite differences on the logit). Let me include it where available.\n\nFor the directions, I should also include:\n- **W_U_edible**: the lm_head's row for \"edible\" token\n- This is the \"readout direction\" — how the model reads \"edible\" from the representation\n\nThis is important because it separates:\n- **Readout space** (W_U): how the model decodes the representation\n- **Computation space** (Jacobian): how the model transforms the representation\n\nOK, let me finalize the design and write the code.\n\n**Phase 34 Design:**\n\n34A: **Perturbation Propagation Map**\n- For each model, each concept (apple, dog, hammer), each sampled layer (4-5 layers):\n  - Collect baseline h at ALL layers\n  - For each direction (probe_edible, probe_animacy, W_U_edible, random, PCA_top1):\n    - Inject perturbation at the sampled layer\n    - Collect h at all subsequent layers\n    - Measure: norm of δ at each subsequent layer, cos(δ, direction)\n    - Measure: logit changes (edible, animacy, size)\n  - Compare across concepts → operator consistency\n\n34B: **Effective Jacobian via Finite Differences**\n- For each layer l, compute J_l @ d for key directions d\n- This gives the \"Jacobian-vector product\" — how the layer transforms each direction\n- Key metrics:\n  - Amplification: ||J_l @ d|| / ||d||\n  - Direction change: cos(J_l @ d, d)\n  - Cross-concept consistency: J_l(h_apple) @ d vs J_l(h_dog) @ d\n\n34C: **Full-Chain Propagation vs Direct Measurement**\n- Chain the layer-wise Jacobians: propagate δ from layer 0 to layer L through all intermediate layers\n- Compare to directly measuring the effect of a perturbation at layer 0 on layer L\n- If they match → the model is approximately linear\n- If they don't → there's nonlinearity in the composition\n\nActually, 34C might be too similar to 34A. Let me merge them.\n\nLet me simplify to two experiments:\n\n**34A: Perturbation Propagation Map + Operator Consistency**\n- Full propagation map: how does a perturbation at layer l affect all subsequent layers and the output?\n- Operator consistency: does the same perturbation propagate differently for different concepts?\n- This is the core experiment.\n\n**34B: Layer-wise Jacobian Analysis**\n- At each layer, measure J_l @ d for key directions\n- How does each layer transform different directions?\n- Compare probe directions vs random vs PCA → are some directions preferentially preserved/amplified?\n\nLet me now write the code.\n\nFor the implementation, I need:\n1. A function to collect h at all layers in one forward pass\n2. A function to inject a perturbation at a specific layer and collect h at all subsequent layers\n3. Register a hook at the target layer that modifies the output\n2. Run the full forward pass\n3. The hook modifies the output, and subsequent layers process the modified version\n\nThis works because in a standard transformer:\n- Layer 0 processes the input\n- Layer 1 processes Layer 0's output\n- ...\n- Layer L-1 processes Layer L-2's output\n\nIf I modify Layer l's output, Layer l+1 will see the modified version, and so on.\n\nLet me verify this works with a simple test. Actually, I'm confident this works — it's a standard technique.\n\nOne issue: the hook needs to modify the output at the SPECIFIC token position (dep_idx), not all positions. Because we're studying the representation of a specific word.\n\nAlso, I need to be careful about the residual stream. In transformers, the output of a layer is added to the residual stream. So the hook captures the full residual stream, not just the layer's contribution.\n\nWait, let me re-check. In our Phase 33 code, we used:\n```python\ndef hook_fn(module, input, output):\n    if isinstance(output, tuple):\n        captured['h'] = output[0].detach().float().cpu().numpy()\n    else:\n        captured['h'] = output.detach().float().cpu().numpy()\n```\n\nThis captures the OUTPUT of the layer, which in a standard transformer is the residual stream after that layer. So h_l is the full residual stream at position l.\n\nWhen I inject a perturbation at layer l, I'm modifying the residual stream at that point. Subsequent layers will see the modified residual stream.\n\nThis is correct — I want to study how a perturbation to the representation at layer l propagates through the remaining layers.\n\nOK, let me now write the code. I'll create the Phase 34 script.\n\nLet me think about the exact metrics I want to compute:\n\nFor each (concept, source_layer, direction):\n1. **Within-layer propagation**: \n   - δ_l = ε * direction (the injected perturbation)\n   - δ_{l+1} = h_{l+1}(perturbed) - h_{l+1}(baseline)\n   - Amplitude: ||δ_{l+1}|| / ||δ_l|| (how much the perturbation grows/shrinks)\n   - Direction: cos(δ_{l+1}, δ_l) (how much the direction is preserved)\n\n2. **Full-chain propagation**:\n   - For each subsequent layer l': δ_{l'} = h_{l'}(perturbed) - h_{l'}(baseline)\n   - Amplitude ratio: ||δ_{l'}|| / ||δ_l||\n   - Direction: cos(δ_{l'}, δ_l) — does the perturbation stay in the same direction?\n\n3. **Output effect**:\n   - Δlogit_edible, Δlogit_animacy, Δlogit_size\n   - Compare to the direct gradient (from Phase 33)\n\n4. **Operator consistency** (across concepts):\n   - For the same (source_layer, direction), compare the propagation for apple vs dog vs hammer\n   - If similar → the layer is approximately linear (input-independent Jacobian)\n   - If different → the layer is nonlinear (input-dependent Jacobian)\n\nNow, there's a subtlety: when I inject a perturbation at layer l, the subsequent layers see the modified input. But each subsequent layer also has its own Jacobian. So the perturbation at layer l' depends on ALL the Jacobians from l to l'-1 composed.\n\nδ_{l'} ≈ J_{l'-1} · J_{l'-2} · ... · J_l · δ_l\n\nIf the Jacobians are approximately constant (linear regime), then this chain gives us the full causal path.\n\nOK, I think I have a clear enough plan. Let me write the code.\n\nOne more thing: I need to decide on the perturbation magnitude ε. It should be:\n- Small enough that we're in the linear regime (Taylor expansion valid)\n- Large enough that the effect is measurable above numerical noise\n\nA good choice: ε = 0.1 * ||h_l - h_mean||, where h_mean is the mean hidden state. This scales with the typical magnitude of the hidden states.\n\nBut for comparing across layers and models, I might want a fixed ε. Let me use both:\n- Normalized ε = 0.1 (relative to ||h_l - h_mean||)\n- And a fixed ε for comparison\n\nActually, let me use ε = 1.0 as in Phase 33, and also test ε = 0.1 and ε = 0.01 to check linearity.\n\nIf the effect is linear in ε (doubling ε doubles the effect), then we're in the linear regime. If not, there's significant nonlinearity.\n\nOK let me just write the code now. Better.\n\nActually, let me simplify even further. The user said:\n\n> 🔥 1. Jacobian chain（最关键）\n> 🔥 2. feature attribution across layers\n> 🔥 3. representation flow（流）\n\nSo the three key things are:\n1. Jacobian chain\n2. Feature attribution across layers\n3. Representation flow\n\nLet me design experiments that directly address these.\n\n**34A: Jacobian Chain — Layer-wise Perturbation Propagation**\n- At each sampled layer l, inject perturbation δ along direction d\n- Measure the effect at the NEXT layer (l+1) → this is J_l @ d\n- Measure the effect at the FINAL output → this is the total causal effect\n- Build a \"Jacobian chain\" by composing the layer-wise effects\n- Compare the composed chain to the direct measurement\n\n**34B: Feature Attribution Across Layers**\n- At each layer, which directions (probe, PCA, random) have the most causal effect on the output?\n- How does this change across layers?\n- Which layers are \"bottlenecks\" for specific attributes?\n\n**34C: Representation Flow**\n- Track how the hidden state of \"apple\" changes across layers\n- Measure: the trajectory h_0 → h_1 → ... → h_{L-1}\n- Is the trajectory smooth? Does it follow geodesic-like paths?\n- How does the trajectory differ from \"dog\" and \"hammer\"?\n\nFor 34C, I don't need perturbations — I just need to collect h at all layers for each concept and analyze the trajectory. This is simpler and can be done with hooks.\n\nOK, let me finalize the design and write the code. I'll aim for a practical, runnable script.\n\n**Final Phase 34 Design:**\n\n34A: **Perturbation Propagation + Jacobian Chain**\n- For each model, sample 8-10 layers (evenly spaced)\n- For each sampled layer, inject perturbation along:\n  - probe_edible, probe_animacy, random direction\n- For each perturbation, measure:\n  - Effect on next layer's h at the token position\n  - Effect on final output logits (edible, animacy, size)\n- Analysis:\n  - Propagation amplitude and direction preservation\n  - Cross-concept consistency (operator linearity)\n  - Total causal effect map: which layers matter most for edible?\n\n34B: **Representation Flow**\n- Collect h at ALL layers for apple, dog, hammer\n- Analyze the trajectory:\n  - Layer-to-layer displacement: h_{l+1} - h_l\n  - Cumulative displacement from L0\n  - Speed (norm of displacement) at each layer\n  - How attributes (probe predictions) change across layers\n- This gives us the \"flow\" of representations through the model\n\nI'll combine these into one script.\n\nLet me now write the code.\n\nActually, I want to make sure the perturbation injection works correctly. Let me think about the implementation more carefully.\n\nWhen I inject a perturbation at layer l, I'm modifying the output of layer l (the residual stream). The subsequent layers will process the modified residual stream.\n\nBut here's a subtlety: in a transformer, each layer does:\n```\nh_l = h_{l-1} + Attention(h_{l-1}) + MLP(h_{l-1} + Attention(h_{l-1}))\n```\n\nActually, with layer norm:\n```\nh_l = h_{l-1} + Attention(LayerNorm(h_{l-1}))\nh_l = h_l + MLP(LayerNorm(h_l))\n```\n\nWait, the output of the layer IS h_l (the full residual stream after that layer). So when I register a hook on the layer, I get h_l.\n\nWhen I modify h_l by adding δ, the next layer sees h_l + δ as its input. This is correct.\n\nBut there's a subtle issue with LayerNorm. If I modify h_l, then LayerNorm(h_l + δ) ≠ LayerNorm(h_l) + δ/LayerNorm_scale. The LayerNorm will rescale the perturbation. This means the effective Jacobian includes the LayerNorm's effect.\n\nThis is actually fine — we want to measure the REAL propagation, including LayerNorm's effect. The Jacobian of the full layer (including LayerNorm) is what we care about.\n\nOK, let me write the code now. I'll be practical and efficient.\n\nFor the implementation, I'll:\n1. Collect baseline h at all layers for each concept (using hooks)\n2. For each perturbation test, inject and measure (using a hook that modifies the output)\n3. Save all results to JSON\n\nHere's my implementation plan:\n\n```python\ndef collect_all_layer_hs(model, tokenizer, device, concept, template, n_layers):\n    \"\"\"Collect h at all layers for a given concept and template\"\"\"\n    sent = template.format(word=concept)\n    toks = tokenizer(sent, return_tensors=\"pt\").to(device)\n    tokens_list = [safe_decode(tokenizer, t) for t in toks.input_ids[0].tolist()]\n    dep_idx = find_token_index(tokens_list, concept)\n    \n    layers = get_layers(model)\n    captured = {}\n    \n    def make_hook(li):\n        def hook(module, input, output):\n            if isinstance(output, tuple):\n                captured[f'L{li}'] = output[0].detach().float().cpu().numpy()\n            else:\n                captured[f'L{li}'] = output.detach().float().cpu().numpy()\n        return hook\n    \n    hooks = []\n    for li in range(n_layers):\n        hooks.append(layers[li].register_forward_hook(make_hook(li)))\n    \n    with torch.no_grad():\n        _ = model(**toks)\n    \n    for h in hooks:\n        h.remove()\n    \n    # Extract h at dep_idx position for each layer\n    result = {}\n    for li in range(n_layers):\n        key = f'L{li}'\n        if key in captured:\n            result[li] = captured[key][0, dep_idx, :]  # [d_model]\n    \n    return result\n\ndef inject_perturbation_and_measure(model, tokenizer, device, concept, template,\n                                     layer_idx, direction, epsilon, n_layers,\n                                     edible_token_ids, animacy_token_ids):\n    \"\"\"Inject perturbation at layer_idx and measure effects\"\"\"\n    sent = template.format(word=concept)\n    toks = tokenizer(sent, return_tensors=\"pt\").to(device)\n    tokens_list = [safe_decode(tokenizer, t) for t in toks.input_ids[0].tolist()]\n    dep_idx = find_token_index(tokens_list, concept)\n    \n    layers = get_layers(model)\n    captured = {}\n    \n    def make_hook(li):\n        def hook(module, input, output):\n            if isinstance(output, tuple):\n                captured[f'L{li}'] = output[0].detach().float().cpu().numpy()\n            else:\n                captured[f'L{li}'] = output.detach().float().cpu().numpy()\n        return hook\n    \n    # Hook for injection at target layer\n    def inject_hook(module, input, output):\n        if isinstance(output, tuple):\n            out = output[0].clone()\n        else:\n            out = output.clone()\n        # Add perturbation at dep_idx position\n        delta = torch.tensor(epsilon * direction, dtype=out.dtype, device=device)\n        out[0, dep_idx, :] += delta\n        if isinstance(output, tuple):\n            return (out,) + output[1:]\n        return out\n    \n    hooks = []\n    for li in range(n_layers):\n        if li == layer_idx:\n            hooks.append(layers[li].register_forward_hook(inject_hook))\n        else:\n            hooks.append(layers[li].register_forward_hook(make_hook(li)))\n    \n    with torch.no_grad():\n        outputs = model(**toks)\n        logits = outputs.logits\n    \n    for h in hooks:\n        h.remove()\n    \n    # Extract results\n    result = {}\n    for li in range(n_layers):\n        key = f'L{li}'\n        if key in captured:\n            result[li] = captured[key][0, dep_idx, :]\n    \n    # Logit changes (will compare to baseline outside this function)\n    logit_edible = float(logits[0, -1, edible_token_ids].mean()) if len(edible_token_ids) > 0 else 0.0\n    logit_animacy = float(logits[0, -1, animacy_token_ids].mean()) if len(animacy_token_ids) > 0 else 0.0\n    \n    return result, logit_edible, logit_animacy\n```\n\nHmm, this approach requires running the model twice for each perturbation test (once for baseline, once for perturbed). And for each run, we register hooks at all layers. This is correct but potentially slow.\n\nLet me optimize: for the baseline, I only need to collect h at all layers ONCE per concept. Then for each perturbation, I only need to compare to the baseline.\n\nActually, let me think about memory. For each forward pass with hooks at all layers:\n- Each hook captures a [seq_len, d_model] tensor\n- For d_model=2560 and seq_len~10, that's ~100KB per layer\n- For 36 layers, that's ~3.6MB total\n- This is fine\n\nFor the perturbed forward pass, same thing. So each test requires ~7.2MB of CPU memory. Totally fine.\n\nThe main bottleneck is the number of forward passes. Let me estimate:\n- 3 concepts × 1 baseline = 3 forward passes\n- 3 concepts × 5 directions × ~10 layers × 2 ε values = 300 forward passes\n- Total: ~303 forward passes per model\n\nAt ~0.3-0.5s per forward pass (small sequence), that's ~90-150 seconds per model. Manageable.\n\nBut wait, I also need to train probes at each layer. This requires collecting h for all concepts at each layer. I can do this during the baseline collection.\n\nActually, I realize the probe training was already done in Phase 33. But Phase 33 only tested 4 layers. For Phase 34, I need probes at ALL sampled layers.\n\nLet me adjust: I'll collect h for all concepts at each sampled layer and train probes on the fly. This is fast since it's just Ridge regression.\n\nOK, I think I've thought about this enough. Let me write the actual code now.\n\nLet me also think about what makes this experiment REALLY valuable. The key insight from the user's critique is:\n\n**We're not modeling the PATH. We need to understand how information flows through the model, not just what the endpoints look like.**\n\nThe perturbation propagation experiment directly addresses this. By injecting a perturbation at each layer and measuring its effect at subsequent layers, we build a complete picture of the \"causal path.\"\n\nThe most striking result we could find:\n1. **Different attributes have different propagation patterns**: edible perturbations might be amplified at certain layers, animacy at others\n2. **The propagation is NOT input-independent**: the same perturbation propagates differently for apple vs dog → the model IS nonlinear\n3. **Probe directions get suppressed during propagation**: if probe directions are in the null space of the Jacobian, they get killed → this explains why probes are non-causal\n4. **The Jacobian chain reveals bottlenecks**: certain layers are \"information gates\" that control what passes through\n\nThese would be truly novel findings that advance our understanding.\n\nLet me write the code now.\n\nOne more thing: I should test with MULTIPLE ε values to check if the propagation is linear (effect proportional to ε) or nonlinear (effect not proportional to ε). If nonlinear, then the Jacobian approximation breaks down.\n\nOK, writing the code now...\n\nActually, let me think about one more thing. The user's critique says:\n\n> 梯度只是\"局部最陡上升方向\" ∇_h ℓ\n> 它表示：在当前点对当前 logit 的一阶变化\n> 但它不是：全局控制方向 / 稳定语义方向 / 可解释特征方向\n\nThis is correct. But there's a nuance: the gradient IS the correct local causal direction. The question is whether it's also a good GLOBAL causal direction.\n\nIf the model is approximately linear (as Phase 33 suggests for mid-deep layers), then the gradient should be approximately constant across different inputs. In that case, it WOULD be a good global causal direction.\n\nIf the model is significantly nonlinear, then the gradient varies across inputs, and we can't use it as a global direction.\n\nThe perturbation propagation experiment directly tests this: if the same perturbation has similar effects for different concepts, the model is approximately linear. If not, it's nonlinear.\n\nOK, I'm going to write the code now. No more deliberation.\n\nLet me structure it as a single comprehensive script that:\n1. Collects baseline representations at all layers for all concepts\n2. Trains probes at all layers\n3. Runs perturbation propagation tests\n4. Analyzes the results\n5. Let me write the code.\n\nWait, I realize there's another issue. When I inject a perturbation at layer l using a hook, the hook modifies the output of layer l. But I also have a hook at layer l to capture the output. The order of hooks matters — I need the modification hook to run BEFORE the capture hook.\n\nIn PyTorch, when you register multiple forward hooks on the same module, they are called in the order they were registered. So I need to register the inject hook FIRST, then the capture hook.\n\nActually, this is tricky. Let me think about it more carefully.\n\nWhen I register a hook with `register_forward_hook`, the hook is called AFTER the module's forward pass. The hook receives (module, input, output) and can return a modified output.\n\nIf I register two hooks on the same module:\n1. Hook 1: inject perturbation (modifies output)\n2. Hook 2: capture output\n\nThe hooks are called in registration order. Hook 1 returns the modified output. Then Hook 2 receives the modified output.\n\nWait, actually in PyTorch, each hook independently receives the original output. The modified output from one hook is NOT passed to the next hook. Each hook gets the same input.\n\nNo wait, let me check. From PyTorch docs:\n\n> The hook will be called every time after forward() has computed an output. It should have the following signature: hook(module, input, output) -> None or modified output\n\nAnd:\n\n> If the hook returns a modified output, that will be used as the output of the module instead of the original output.\n\nBut what about multiple hooks? Let me check...\n\nFrom PyTorch source code, when multiple hooks are registered, they are called in order, and each subsequent hook receives the output from the previous hook (if it was modified). So:\n\n1. Hook 1 receives original output, returns modified output\n2. Hook 2 receives Hook 1's modified output, can further modify or just capture\n\nThis means I can register:\n1. First hook: inject perturbation (modify output)\n2. Second hook: capture output (no modification)\n\nAnd the captured output will be the modified one. \n\nBut wait, I also want to capture the UNMODIFIED output at the injection layer (to compute the baseline). So I need a separate baseline forward pass.\n\nActually, I already planned to do a separate baseline pass. So:\n1. Baseline pass: collect h at all layers (no perturbation)\n2. Perturbed pass: inject perturbation at layer l, collect h at all layers (including the perturbed output at layer l)\n\nThen δ_{l'} = h_{l'}(perturbed) - h_{l'}(baseline) for each l'.\n\nOK this is clear. Let me write the code.\n\nOne more consideration: for the perturbation at layer l, I want to measure the effect at layer l+1, l+2, etc. But what about the effect at layer l itself?\n\nAt layer l, the perturbed h_l = baseline h_l + ε * direction. So δ_l = ε * direction (exactly what we injected). This is trivial — we know it.\n\nAt layer l+1, δ_{l+1} = h_{l+1}(perturbed) - h_{l+1}(baseline). This is the Jacobian-vector product: J_l @ (ε * direction).\n\nAt layer l+2, δ_{l+2} = J_{l+1} @ δ_{l+1} = J_{l+1} @ J_l @ (ε * direction).\n\nAnd so on.\n\nThis is exactly the Jacobian chain! By measuring δ at each subsequent layer, we're implicitly computing the Jacobian chain applied to our perturbation.\n\nBeautiful. This is exactly what we need.\n\nNow, there's one subtlety: the Jacobian J_l depends on the INPUT to layer l. If I perturb h_l, then the input to layer l+1 is h_l + δ. The Jacobian J_{l+1} is evaluated at h_l + δ, not at h_l. So:\n\nδ_{l+2} = J_{l+1}(h_l + δ) @ δ_{l+1} ≈ J_{l+1}(h_l) @ δ_{l+1} + O(δ²)\n\nFor small δ, this is approximately the linear chain. For large δ, there are nonlinear effects.\n\nBy testing multiple ε values (0.1 and 1.0), I can check if the propagation is approximately linear. If δ_{l'} scales linearly with ε, we're in the linear regime. If not, there's significant nonlinearity.\n\nOK, I'm definitely ready to write the code now. I'll aim for a complete, runnable script that produces meaningful results.\n\nLet me start writing...\n\ndef find_token_index(tokens, target_word):\n    # same as Phase 33\n\ndef collect_hs_with_hooks(model, tokenizer, device, words, layer_indices, template):\n    \"\"\"Collect h at specified layers for multiple words using one forward pass per word\"\"\"\n    layers = get_layers(model)\n    results = {}  # {word: {layer_idx: h_vector}}\n    \n    for word in words:\n        sent = template.format(word=word)\n        toks = tokenizer(sent, return_tensors=\"pt\").to(device)\n        tokens_list = [safe_decode(tokenizer, t) for t in toks.input_ids[0].tolist()]\n        dep_idx = find_token_index(tokens_list, word)\n        \n        if dep_idx < 0:\n            continue\n        \n        captured = {}\n        def make_hook(li):\n            def hook(module, input, output):\n                if isinstance(output, tuple):\n                    captured[li] = output[0].detach().float().cpu().numpy()\n                else:\n                    captured[li] = output.detach().float().cpu().numpy()\n            return hook\n        \n        hooks = []\n        for li in layer_indices:\n            if li < len(layers):\n                hooks.append(layers[li].register_forward_hook(make_hook(li)))\n        \n        with torch.no_grad():\n            _ = model(**toks)\n        \n        for h in hooks:\n            h.remove()\n        \n        word_result = {}\n        for li in layer_indices:\n            if li in captured:\n                word_result[li] = captured[li][0, dep_idx, :]\n        \n        results[word] = word_result\n    \n    return results\n\nWait, this approach registers hooks only at the specified layers, not all layers. For the propagation map, I need hooks at ALL layers.\n\nLet me restructure. For the baseline collection and perturbation tests, I need hooks at all layers. But for probe training, I only need hooks at the source layers.\n\nLet me separate these:\n\n1. **Probe training**: collect h at source layers only → efficient\n2. **Baseline + perturbation**: collect h at ALL layers → more expensive but necessary\n\nFor the baseline collection, I'll register hooks at all layers. For the perturbation tests, same.\n\nOK let me just write the full code. I'll aim for clarity over efficiency.\n\nLet me write a helper function that collects h at all layers:\n\ndef collect_all_layer_hs(model, tokenizer, device, word, template, n_layers, dep_idx=None):\n    \"\"\"Collect h at ALL layers for a single word\"\"\"\n    sent = template.format(word=word)\n    toks = tokenizer(sent, return_tensors=\"pt\").to(device)\n    tokens_list = [safe_decode(tokenizer, t) for t in toks.input_ids[0].tolist()]\n    \n    if dep_idx is None:\n        dep_idx = find_token_index(tokens_list, word)\n    \n    if dep_idx < 0:\n        return None\n    \n    layers = get_layers(model)\n    captured = {}\n    \n    def make_hook(li):\n        def hook(module, input, output):\n            if isinstance(output, tuple):\n                captured[li] = output[0].detach().float().cpu().numpy()\n            else:\n                captured[li] = output.detach().float().cpu().numpy()\n        return hook\n    \n    hooks = []\n    for li in range(n_layers):\n        hooks.append(layers[li].register_forward_hook(make_hook(li)))\n    \n    with torch.no_grad():\n        _ = model(**toks)\n    \n    for h in hooks:\n        h.remove()\n    \n    result = {}\n    for li in range(n_layers):\n        if li in captured:\n            result[li] = captured[li][0, dep_idx, :]\n    \n    return result\n\nAnd for the perturbation test:\n\ndef inject_and_propagate(model, tokenizer, device, word, template, \n                         source_layer, direction, epsilon, n_layers, dep_idx=None):\n    \"\"\"Inject perturbation at source_layer and collect h at all subsequent layers\"\"\"\n    sent = template.format(word=word)\n    toks = tokenizer(sent, return_tensors=\"pt\").to(device)\n    tokens_list = [safe_decode(tokenizer, t) for t in toks.input_ids[0].tolist()]\n    \n    if dep_idx is None:\n        dep_idx = find_token_index(tokens_list, word)\n    \n    if dep_idx < 0:\n        return None\n    \n    layers = get_layers(model)\n    captured = {}\n    \n    def make_hook(li):\n        def hook(module, input, output):\n            if isinstance(output, tuple):\n                captured[li] = output[0].detach().float().cpu().numpy()\n            else:\n                captured[li] = output.detach().float().cpu().numpy()\n        return hook\n    \n    def inject_hook(module, input, output):\n        if isinstance(output, tuple):\n            out = output[0].clone()\n        else:\n            out = output.clone()\n        delta = torch.tensor(epsilon * direction, dtype=out.dtype, device=device)\n        out[0, dep_idx, :] += delta\n        if isinstance(output, tuple):\n            return (out,) + output[1:]\n        return out\n    \n    hooks = []\n    for li in range(n_layers):\n        if li == source_layer:\n            hooks.append(layers[li].register_forward_hook(inject_hook))\n        else:\n            hooks.append(layers[li].register_forward_hook(make_hook(li)))\n    \n    with torch.no_grad():\n        outputs = model(**toks)\n        logits = outputs.logits[0, -1, :]  # last token logits\n    \n logits.detach().float().cpu().numpy()\n\nThis looks good. I'm going to produce a complete script.\n\nActually, I just realized there's another important thing: for the perturbation test, I need to be careful about which token position to modify and measure.\n\nIn our setup:\n- Input: \"The apple is here\" → tokens: [\"The\", \" apple\", \" is\", \" here\"]\n- dep_idx = 1 (the \"apple\" token)\n- We inject perturbation at position dep_idx in the hidden state\n- We measure the effect at position dep_idx in subsequent layers\n- We measure the logit change at position dep_idx\n\nThis is consistent with what we've been doing. For each concept, compute the trajectory: h_0 → h_1 → ... → h_{L-1}\n2. Compute displacement norms: ||h_{l+1} - h_l|| for each l\n3. Compute cumulative displacement: ||h_l - h_0|| for each l\n4. Compute inter-concept distances: ||h_l(apple) - h_l(dog)|| for each l\n5. Compute probe predictions at each layer: how do they evolve?\n\nThis gives us the \"flow\" of representations through the model.\n\nOK, I'm going to write the complete script now. It will be long but comprehensive.\n\nLet me also think about the output format. I want the results to be easily analyzable. I'll save:\n1. A summary JSON with key metrics (propagation maps, consistency scores, etc.)\n2. Detailed results for each perturbation test\n\nThe summary should include:\n- propagation_map: {source_layer: {direction: {epsilon: {target_layer: {delta_norm_ratio, delta_cos}}}}}\n- logit_effects: {concept: {source_layer: {direction: {epsilon: {edible_delta, animacy_delta}}}}}\n- operator_consistency: {source_layer: {direction: {consistency_across_concepts}}}\n- representation_flow: {concept: {layer: {displacement_norm, cumulative_norm, probe_predictions}}}\n- inter_concept_distances: {layer: {pair: distance}}\n\nOK, writing the code now.\n\nActually, I realize I should make this more practical. Instead of saving h vectors for all layers (which would be huge), I should compute and save only the metrics I need.\n\nFor each perturbation test, I need:\n- δ at each subsequent layer (to compute norms and cosines)\n- δ at the output (logit changes)\n\nI compute these on the fly and save only the summary metrics.\n\nLet me restructure the code:\n\n```python\ndef run_perturbation_test(model, tokenizer, device, concept, template,\n                          source_layer, direction, epsilon, n_layers,\n                          baseline_hs, baseline_logits, \n                          edible_token_ids, animacy_token_ids, size_token_ids):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run a single perturbation test and return summary metrics.\n    \n    Returns:\n        dict with:\n        - propagation: {target_layer: {delta_norm, delta_cos_orig, delta_cos_prev}}\n        - logit_deltas: {edible, animacy, size}\n    \"\"\"\n    # Get baseline h at source layer\n    h_source_baseline = baseline_hs[concept][source_layer]\n    \n    # Inject perturbation and collect h at all layers\n    perturbed_result, perturbed_logits = inject_and_propagate(\n        model, tokenizer, device, concept, template,\n        source_layer, direction, epsilon, n_layers\n    )\n    \n    if perturbed_result is None:\n        return None\n    \n    # Compute propagation metrics\n    propagation = {}\n    prev_delta = None\n    for target_layer in range(source_layer + 1, n_layers):\n        if target_layer not in perturbed_result or target_layer not in baseline_hs[concept]:\n            continue\n        \n        h_target_perturbed = perturbed_result[target_layer]\n        h_target_baseline = baseline_hs[concept][target_layer]\n        \n        delta = h_target_perturbed - h_target_baseline\n        delta_norm = float(np.linalg.norm(delta))\n        \n        # Cosine with original direction\n        delta_cos_orig = float(compute_cos(delta, direction))\n        \n        # Cosine with previous layer's delta\n        if prev_delta is not None:\n            delta_cos_prev = float(compute_cos(delta, prev_delta))\n        else:\n            delta_cos_prev = None\n        \n        propagation[target_layer] = {\n            'delta_norm': delta_norm,\n            'delta_norm_ratio': delta_norm / max(float(np.linalg.norm(direction * epsilon)), 1e-10),\n            'delta_cos_orig': delta_cos_orig,\n            'delta_cos_prev': delta_cos_prev,\n        }\n        \n        prev_delta = delta\n    \n    # Compute logit deltas\n    logit_deltas = {}\n    for attr_name, token_ids in [('edible', edible_token_ids), \n                                   ('animacy', animacy_token_ids),\n                                   ('size', size_token_ids)]:\n        if len(token_ids) > 0:\n            baseline_logit = float(baseline_logits[concept][attr_name])\n            perturbed_logit = float(perturbed_logits[token_ids].mean())\n            logit_deltas[attr_name] = perturbed_logit - baseline_logit\n    \n    return {\n        'propagation': propagation,\n        'logit_deltas': logit_deltas,\n    }\n```\n\nThis is cleaner. The baseline_hs and baseline_logits are pre-computed.\n\nOK, I think I have a clear enough plan. import json

for model in ['qwen3', 'glm4', 'deepseek7b']:
    with open(f'ccml_phase34_expA_{model}_results.json', 'r') as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    
    print(f'\n=== {model} Logit Effects (edible_delta) ===')
    for concept in ['apple', 'dog', 'hammer']:
        print(f'  {concept}:')
        for src_layer in sorted(data['logit_effects'].get(concept, {}).keys(), key=lambda x: int(x)):
            for dir_name in ['probe_edible', 'lm_head_edible', 'random']:
                if dir_name in data['logit_effects'][concept][src_layer]:
                    eps_data = data['logit_effects'][concept][src_layer][dir_name]
                    for eps in ['0.1', '1.0']:
                        if eps in eps_data and 'edible' in eps_data[eps]:
                            delta = eps_data[eps]['edible']
                            print(f'    L{src_layer} {dir_name} eps={eps}: edible_delta={delta:.3f}')
                    break  # only print once per layer
    
    # Operator consistency summary
    print(f'\n  Operator Consistency (CV < 0.3 = linear):')
    for src_layer in sorted(data['operator_consistency'].keys(), key=lambda x: int(x)):
        cv_vals = []
        for key, val in data['operator_consistency'][src_layer].items():
            if 'eps1.0' in key:
                cv_vals.append(val['cv'])
        if cv_vals:
            mean_cv = sum(cv_vals) / len(cv_vals)
            print(f'    L{src_layer}: mean_CV={mean_cv:.3f} ({\"LINEAR\" if mean_cv < 0.3 else \"NONLINEAR\"})')
"
, disapprove)

## Phase 34 总结

### 批评分析：5条全部正确

| 批评 | 评判 | 核心修正 |
|------|------|---------|
| gradient≠因果方向 | ✔ | gradient只是局部决策边界方向，input-dependent |
| probe负效应≠无用 | ✔ | probe捕捉E[y\|h](相关性)，gradient捕捉∂logit/∂h(变化率)，两者本质不同 |
| 病态矩阵解释过度 | ✔ | 高cond在高维是常态，零空间不是唯一解释 |
| 残差≠非线性语义 | ✔ | 残差可能含未拟合线性结构/噪声 |
| 内在维度≠流形 | ✔ | 可能是clustered cloud/mixture of subspaces |

### Phase 34 三大发现

**1. 模型是近似线性算子(Qwen3/GLM4)**
- 扰动传播跨概念高度一致(CV<0.15) → Jacobian近似input-independent
- DS7B中间层例外(CV>0.3) → 非线性更强

**2. 所有方向在Jacobian链中被快速旋转**
- cos(original, delta)从0.9降到0.1 → 不存在贯穿全模型的"语义方向"
- 扰动被放大(amp=4-25x)但方向被旋转 → 模型是"旋转放大器"

**3. lm_head方向是真正的因果管道**
- W_U方向对logit效应远超probe方向(5-9倍)
- 因果=Jacobian链传播 + W_U读出，不是表示中的"内在方向"

### 最严格的审视

**硬伤**:
1. **probe R²≈1.000太完美** → 70概念3属性太容易线性可分，不能说明问题
2. **lm_head"发现"可能是trivial的** → W_U直接连接h和logit，当然沿它走有效
3. **3个测试概念泛化不足** → 需要边界案例(如salmon: edible+animate)
4. **DS7B的非线性可能是8bit量化artifact**

### 突破瓶颈的第一性原理

核心洞察：**语义不是h中的方向，而是Jacobian链对信号的变换结果**。模型 = 旋转放大器链 + 读出矩阵(W_U)。

**下一阶段大任务**：从"Jacobian链的宏观行为"深入到"Jacobian的微观结构"——

1. **逐层Jacobian奇异值谱**：每层的J_l有多少有效维度？哪些方向被放大/抑制？
2. **Jacobian链的旋转累积规律**：是随机游走还是确定性旋转？能否预测？
3. **因果归因图**：哪个层对哪个属性最关键？能否从Jacobian链推导出层间的"因果贡献"？
4. **跨模型Jacobian结构比较**：Qwen3/GLM4的线性Jacobian vs DS7B的非线性Jacobian，结构差异在哪？

- 这个项目的目标是实现agi，核心思路：深度神经网络提取了大脑中的某种数学结构，产生了语言能力，语言能力包括了语义，逻辑，语法等维度，有完整规则，说明背后有某种数学结构，这个数学结构很可能超过了现有认知，所以不要轻易使用任何统计学方法和高级数学理论，只进行基础分析，当完成大量拼图后，让结论自然体现出来.

如果需要进行模型测试，请基于cuda使用本地硬盘上的qwen3，GLM4 8bit和DS7B 8bit完成测试，注意测试完一个再测试另外一个，避免gpu内存溢出。
设计测试方案时，不能样本太少导致判断错误，也不能太大导致时间太长。而且要避免出现k为固定值，得到ratio=2.2，把特殊情况当初普遍结论的情况。

所有测试脚本和都添加到  /tests/glm5/ 下面，所有临时脚本添加到 /tests/glm5_temp/ 下面。

关键问题（有明显逻辑漏洞的地方）
❗问题1：gradient = 因果方向 → 这个推断不成立（非常关键）

你现在的核心跳跃是：

梯度方向 ≈ 因果方向

但实际：

❌ 梯度只是“局部最陡上升方向”
∇
h
	

ℓ

它表示：

在当前点
对当前 logit
的一阶变化

但它不是：

全局控制方向
稳定语义方向
可解释特征方向
🚨 举个关键问题：

梯度是 input-dependent 的：

apple 的 gradient ≠ dog 的 gradient
同一 token 在不同上下文 gradient 也不同

👉 所以你现在实际上发现的是：

“模型的决策方向是高度依赖输入的”

这叫：

local decision boundary geometry

不是：

“存在一个真实语义方向”

✔ 正确说法应该是：

gradient 是局部因果敏感方向，不是语义方向

❗问题2：probe 方向负效应 ≠ “完全无用”

你现在的结论：

probe 是假的方向

这个太强了。

更准确是：

✔ probe 捕捉的是：
E[y∣h]

而不是：

dh
dy
	

这两者区别是核心：
类型	含义
probe	静态相关性
gradient	局部变化率

👉 在非线性系统中，这两个可以完全不一致

甚至：

correlation 正
derivative 负

👉 这是完全可能的（非单调函数）

❗问题3：关于“病态矩阵解释一切”——过度解释

你说：

A condition number 很大 → 零空间解释 probe 差异

这里有两个问题：

⚠️ (1) 高 condition number 在高维很常见

d_model ~ 4000：

几乎所有 learned matrix 都会 condition number 很大
这不是特殊现象

👉 所以不能直接用它解释结构

⚠️ (2) A⁻ᵀw ≠ w 的原因不止零空间

还包括：

非正交基
多解性（overparameterized）
噪声拟合
数据分布变化

👉 你现在把“零空间”作为唯一解释 → 过拟合解释

❗问题4：残差 = 非线性语义载体 → 证据不够

你看到：

residual 有高 R²

但问题是：

❌ residual ≠ 非线性语义

它可能是：

未被线性模型拟合的线性结构
噪声 + bias
局部曲率

👉 你没有证明：

r=f
nonlinear
	

(h)

只是证明：

linearmodel不够
❗问题5：内在维度 ≠ 流形（经典误区）

你现在推：

局部维度低 → 流形

但实际上：

❌ 还可能是：
clustered high-dim cloud
mixture of subspaces
anisotropic Gaussian

👉 真正流形需要：

光滑性（smoothness）
可微结构
geodesic consistency

你现在都没测

🚧 三、真正的瓶颈（最关键）
🧱 瓶颈1：你在混淆三个层级

这是目前最大的结构问题：

1️⃣ 表示空间（representation）
h 是什么结构
2️⃣ 解码空间（readout）
probe / W_U
3️⃣ 因果路径（computation）
h → h → logits

👉 你现在把：

probe（读出）
gradient（最后一层）
表示结构

混在一起解释

🧱 瓶颈2：你没有建模“路径”，只看端点

你现在做的是：

h
L
	

→logits

但真实机制是：

h
L
	

→h
L+1
	

→...→logits

👉 关键缺失：

中间层 Jacobian chain
信息如何逐层流动
🧱 瓶颈3：你还在“找坐标”，但系统是“算子”

你已经接近正确方向了：

模型不是在存“属性坐标”，而是在执行变换

但还没完全跳出来。

🧠 四、你现在最接近的真实结构

我帮你把目前最靠谱的版本写出来：

✔ 更合理的模型（Phase 33修正版）
1. 表示空间:
   h ∈ R^d 是高度冗余、分布式编码

2. 层间:
   h_{l+1} ≈ A_l h_l + 非线性残差
   - 中深层: A_l 主导
   - 浅层: 非线性主导

3. 属性:
   不是方向，而是：
   g(h) = 复杂非线性函数

4. probe:
   学的是 E[g(h) | h] 的线性近似

5. gradient:
   ∇h logit = 局部决策边界方向
   ≠ 全局语义结构

6. 因果:
   是路径上的 Jacobian chain
   不是单层方向
🚀 五、下一步真正该做什么（关键突破点）

你已经不该再：

❌ 找方向
❌ 找子空间
❌ 做线性probe

✅ 应该转向三个关键问题：
🔥 1. Jacobian chain（最关键）

测：

∂h
L
	

∂h
L+1
	

	


然后：

连乘 Jacobian
看信息如何传播

👉 这才是“因果路径”

🔥 2. feature attribution across layers

不是：

h → logit

而是：

哪些维度在每一层被放大/抑制
🔥 3. representation flow（流）

你要看的是：

表示如何在空间中“移动”

而不是：

它在哪个方向

🧾 最终总结（一句话）

👉 你现在最重要的突破是：

已经证明：语义不是方向，也不是子空间，而是一个通过层间变换实现的分布式计算过程

但当前最大问题是：

❗把“局部梯度”误当成“全局因果结构”，以及用线性工具解释本质非线性系统。                   分析以上内容是否正确，综合正确的部分和当前进展，继续完成任务

Arguments: {"command":"cd d:\\Ai2050\\TransformerLens-Project\\tests\\glm5_temp && python -c \"\nimport json\n\nfor model in ['qwen3', 'glm4', 'deepseek7b']:\n    with open(f'ccml_phase34_expA_{model}_results.json', 'r') as f:\n        data = json.load(f)\n    \n    print(f'\\n=== {model} Logit Effects (edible_delta) ===')\n    for concept in ['apple', 'dog', 'hammer']:\n        print(f'  {concept}:')\n        for src_layer in sorted(data['logit_effects'].get(concept, {}).keys(), key=lambda x: int(x)):\n            for dir_name in ['probe_edible', 'lm_head_edible', 'random']:\n                if dir_name in data['logit_effects'][concept][src_layer]:\n                    eps_data = data['logit_effects'][concept][src_layer][dir_name]\n                    for eps in ['0.1', '1.0']:\n                        if eps in eps_data and 'edible' in eps_data[eps]:\n                            delta = eps_data[eps]['edible']\n                            print(f'    L{src_layer} {dir_name} eps={eps}: edible_delta={delta:.3f}')\n                    break  # only print once per layer\n    \n    # Operator consistency summary\n    print(f'\\n  Operator Consistency (CV < 0.3 = linear):')\n    for src_layer in sorted(data['operator_consistency'].keys(), key=lambda x: int(x)):\n        cv_vals = []\n        for key, val in data['operator_consistency'][src_layer].items():\n            if 'eps1.0' in key:\n                cv_vals.append(val['cv'])\n        if cv_vals:\n            mean_cv = sum(cv_vals) / len(cv_vals)\n            print(f'    L{src_layer}: mean_CV={mean_cv:.3f} ({\\\"LINEAR\\\" if mean_cv < 0.3 else \\\"NONLINEAR\\\"})')\n\"\n","requires_approval":false,"explanation":"分析三个模型的logit效应和算子一致性"}

Arguments: {"filePath":"d:\\Ai2050\\TransformerLens-Project\\research\\glm5\\docs\\AGI_GLM5_MEMO.md","old_str":"[Phase 33关键验证时间标记: 2026年05月04日22时21分]\n\n\n\n","new_str":"[Phase 33关键验证时间标记: 2026年05月04日22时21分]\n\n## Phase 33批评分析与修正 [2026-05-04 23:32]\n\n**用户提供的5条批评, 全部正确, 已采纳:**\n\n### 问题1: gradient ≠ 因果方向 ✔完全正确\n\n- gradient = ∇_h ℓ 只是\"局部最陡上升方向\"\n- apple的gradient ≠ dog的gradient → input-dependent\n- 正确说法: gradient = 局部决策边界方向(local decision boundary geometry), 不是稳定语义方向\n- Phase 33把\"梯度方向≈因果方向\"是逻辑跳跃 → 已修正\n\n### 问题2: probe负效应 ≠ 完全无用 ✔完全正确\n\n- probe捕捉 E[y|h] (静态相关性/P(edible|h))\n- gradient捕捉 ∂logit/∂h (局部变化率)\n- 在非线性系统中, correlation正而derivative负是完全可能的\n- Probe不是\"假方向\", 而是测量了不同的东西\n\n### 问题3: 病态矩阵解释过度 ✔完全正确\n\n- d_model~4000时几乎所有learned matrix都高condition number → 不是特殊现象\n- A^{-T}w≠w的原因不止零空间: 非正交基/多解性/噪声拟合/数据分布变化\n- 不能把零空间作为唯一解释\n\n### 问题4: 残差 ≠ 非线性语义 ✔完全正确\n\n- 残差可能是: 未被线性模型拟合的线性结构/噪声+bias/局部曲率\n- 只证明了linear_model不够, 没证明r=f_nonlinear(h)\n\n### 问题5: 内在维度 ≠ 流形 ✔完全正确\n\n- 局部低维还可能是: clustered high-dim cloud / mixture of subspaces / anisotropic Gaussian\n- 真正流形需要: 光滑性/可微结构/geodesic consistency — 都未测\n\n### 三个瓶颈(全部准确):\n\n🧱 瓶颈1: 混淆表示空间/解码空间/因果路径\n🧱 瓶颈2: 只看端点(h_L→logits), 没有建模路径(h_L→h_L+1→...→logits)\n🧱 瓶颈3: 还在找\"坐标/方向\", 但系统是\"算子/变换\"\n\n## Phase 34: 从方向到算子 — Jacobian链与扰动传播 [2026-05-04 23:32]\n\n**实验脚本**: `tests/glm5/ccml_phase34_jacobian_chain.py`\n**结果文件**: `tests/glm5_temp/ccml_phase34_exp{A,B}_{qwen3|glm4|deepseek7b}_results.json`\n\n**核心范式转换**: 从\"找哪个方向编码属性\"转向\"测算子如何变换表示\"\n\n### 34A: ★★★★★★★★★ 扰动传播图 — 最关键实验\n\n**实验设计**: 在每层注入扰动δ, 测量对后续所有层和最终输出的影响\n\n- 这等价于隐式测量Jacobian链: δ_{l+k} ≈ J_{l+k-1}·J_{l+k-2}·...·J_l·δ_l\n- 源层: 约10个均匀采样的层\n- 方向: probe_edible, probe_animacy, lm_head_edible, random, random2\n- epsilon: 0.1和1.0 (线性性检验)\n- 概念: apple, dog, hammer\n\n### 34A 关键发现1: ★★★★★ 算子一致性 — 中深层近似线性算子\n\n**Qwen3 (eps=1.0时, 算子一致性CV)**:\n\n| 层 | mean_CV | 状态 |\n|-----|---------|------|\n| L0  | 0.083 | 线性 |\n| L4  | 0.046 | 线性 |\n| L8  | 0.069 | 线性 |\n| L12 | 0.111 | 线性 |\n| L16 | 0.054 | 线性 |\n| L20 | 0.080 | 线性 |\n| L24 | 0.080 | 线性 |\n| L28 | 0.026 | 线性 |\n| L32 | 0.013 | 线性 |\n| L35 | 0.000 | 线性(恒等) |\n\n**Qwen3: 全层CV<0.15 → 扰动传播跨概念高度一致 → Jacobian近似input-independent**\n\n**GLM4 (eps=1.0时)**:\n\n| 层 | mean_CV | 状态 |\n|-----|---------|------|\n| L0  | 0.027 | 线性 |\n| L4  | 0.021 | 线性 |\n| L8  | 0.034 | 线性 |\n| L12 | 0.033 | 线性 |\n| L16 | 0.040 | 线性 |\n| L20 | 0.042 | 线性 |\n| L24 | 0.064 | 线性 |\n| L28 | 0.045 | 线性 |\n| L32 | 0.033 | 线性 |\n| L36 | 0.006 | 线性 |\n| L39 | 0.000 | 线性(恒等) |\n\n**GLM4: 全层CV<0.07 → 比Qwen3更线性!**\n\n**DS7B (eps=1.0时)**:\n\n| 层 | mean_CV | 状态 |\n|-----|---------|------|\n| L0  | 0.160 | 线性 |\n| L3  | 0.230 | 线性 |\n| L6  | 0.311 | ⚠️非线性 |\n| L9  | 0.305 | ⚠️非线性 |\n| L12 | 0.266 | 线性 |\n| L15 | 0.337 | ⚠️非线性 |\n| L18 | 0.366 | ⚠️非线性 |\n| L21 | 0.401 | ⚠️非线性 |\n| L24 | 0.280 | 线性 |\n| L27 | 0.000 | 线性(恒等) |\n\n**★ DS7B: 中间层(L6-L21)CV>0.3 → 显著非线性! 与Qwen3/GLM4不同! ★**\n\n### 34A 关键发现2: ★★★★★ 方向衰减 — 所有方向在传播中快速旋转\n\n**Qwen3, apple, L0注入, probe_edible方向(eps=0.1)**:\n\n| 目标层 | cos(original, delta) | amplification |\n|--------|---------------------|---------------|\n| L1     | 0.89                | 1.12          |\n| L5     | 0.47                | 1.86          |\n| L9     | 0.26                | 2.28          |\n| L13    | 0.25                | 2.10          |\n| L17    | 0.23                | 2.01          |\n| L21    | 0.25                | 2.44          |\n| L25    | 0.25                | 4.09          |\n| L29    | 0.19                | 6.61          |\n| L33    | 0.14                | 10.92         |\n| L35    | 0.11                | 13.84         |\n\n**★★★ 扰动方向在传播中快速旋转! cos从0.89降到0.11 ★★★**\n- probe_edible方向注入L0, 到L35时方向已完全改变\n- 但amplification持续增长(1.12→13.84) → 扰动被放大但方向被旋转\n- 同样趋势出现在random方向: cos从0.84降到0.04\n\n**DS7B, apple, L0注入, 三种方向对比(eps=0.1)**:\n\n| 目标层 | probe_edible cos | lm_head_edible cos | random cos |\n|--------|-----------------|-------------------|------------|\n| L1     | 0.92            | 0.86              | 0.84       |\n| L4     | 0.76            | 0.71              | 0.66       |\n| L7     | 0.60            | 0.51              | 0.45       |\n| L10    | 0.52            | 0.40              | 0.32       |\n| L13    | 0.46            | 0.36              | 0.27       |\n| L16    | 0.43            | 0.32              | 0.22       |\n| L19    | 0.40            | 0.30              | 0.18       |\n| L22    | 0.34            | 0.21              | 0.13       |\n| L25    | 0.26            | 0.18              | 0.11       |\n| L27    | 0.04            | 0.08              | 0.06       |\n\n**三种方向衰减速度: probe_edible > lm_head_edible > random**\n- Probe方向保留最久, 但到最后一层也几乎完全旋转\n- lm_head方向(rotated by W_U)衰减更快\n- random方向衰减最快\n\n### 34A 关键发现3: ★★★★ Amplification模式 — 浅层注入被大幅放大\n\n**Qwen3, apple, eps=1.0, 到最终层的amplification**:\n\n| 源层 | probe_edible amp | random amp | probe_edible cos | random cos |\n|------|-----------------|------------|-----------------|------------|\n| L0   | 4.6             | 24.6       | 0.06            | 0.03       |\n| L4   | 4.4             | 4.5        | 0.10            | 0.05       |\n| L8   | 3.7             | 4.5        | 0.16            | 0.06       |\n| L12  | 4.0             | 3.9        | 0.18            | 0.10       |\n| L16  | 7.1             | 4.3        | 0.07            | 0.14       |\n| L20  | 4.9             | 3.7        | 0.11            | 0.17       |\n| L24  | 2.9             | 2.6        | 0.32            | 0.36       |\n| L28  | 2.3             | 2.0        | 0.47            | 0.52       |\n| L32  | 1.3             | 1.3        | 0.70            | 0.75       |\n| L35  | 1.0             | 1.0        | 1.00            | 1.00       |\n\n**★★★ 发现 ★★★**:\n1. **L0注入random方向amplification=24.6 → 远大于probe方向(4.6)!**\n   - 浅层注入的随机方向被Jacobian链大幅放大\n   - 但方向被完全旋转(cos=0.03)\n2. **深层注入(L28+)amplification接近1.0, | lm_head_edible | random |\n|------|-------------|----------------|--------|\n| L0   | +6.06       | +16.33         | +7.91  |\n| L8   | +8.67       | +14.48         | +12.13 |\n| L16  | +2.18       | +15.67         | +7.99  |\n| L24  | +7.02       | +15.94         | +7.56  |\n| L32  | +5.60       | +23.80         | +7.76  |\n| L35  | +6.36       | +58.36         | +8.29  |\n\n**Qwen3, dog, eps=1.0, edible logit变化**:\n\n| 源层 | probe_edible | lm_head_edible | random |\n|------|-------------|----------------|--------|\n| L0   | -7.19       | +1.70          | -7.04  |\n| L8   | -3.82 +16.33 for apple)**\n   - 说明lm_head方向被Jacobian链很好地保留和传播\n\n### 34A 关键发现5: ★★★ 线性性检验\n\n| 模型 | amplification一致性比例 |\n|------|----------------------|\n| Qwen3 | 421/468 = 90% |\n| GLM4 | 405/522 = 78% 不是某个特定层突然产生的**\n\n### ★★★★★★★★★★★ Phase 34 核心结论 ★★★★★★★★★★★\n\n**结论1: ★★★★★★★ 模型是近似线性算子 (Qwen3/GLM4), 非线性算子(DS7B中间层)**\n\n- Qwen3/GLM4: 扰动传播跨概念高度一致(CV<0.15) → Jacobian近似input-independent\n- DS7B: 中间层(L6-L21)CV>0.3 → Jacobian显著input-dependent → 非线性\n- 这修正了Phase 33的\"梯度是因果方向\"误读: 在Qwen3/GLM4中, Jacobian近似常数, 所以梯度方向确实接近全局因果方向; 但在DS7B中不是\n\n**结论2: ★★★★★★★ 所有方向在Jacobian链中被快速旋转**\n\n- 无论注入probe方向、lm_head方向还是random方向, 经过几层传播后方向都大幅旋转\n- cos(original, delta) 从~0.9降到~0.1 (经过整个模型)\n- → \"属性方向\"在传播中被Jacobian链旋转了 → 不存在贯穿全模型的稳定方向\n\n**结论3: ★★★★★ lm_head方向是真正的\"因果管道\"**\n\n- lm_head_edible方向对edible logit的效应远超probe方向(5-9倍)\n- 即使在浅层注入, lm_head方向也有效\n- → 模型的因果路径是: h → ... → h_{L-1} → W_U → logit\n- W_U(lm_head)定义了\"哪些h分量被读取\", Jacobian链定义了\"如何传播到可读取位置\"\n\n**结论4: ★★★★ 扰动被放大但方向被旋转 — 信号传播的\"旋转放大器\"模型**\n\n- Amplification: 浅层注入→到输出被放大4-25倍\n- 但方向被旋转(cos→0.1)\n- → 模型是\"旋转放大器\": 信号被放大, 但原始方向信息被Jacobian链混合\n- → 任何单一方向都不是\"语义方向\" — 语义是Jacobian链对信号的变换结果\n\n**结论5: ★★★ 表示流是加速的 — 深层变换更剧烈**\n\n- 早期位移小(~15), 中期(~20), 晚期(~100)\n- → 模型在深层对表示做大幅变换 (可能对应\"refinement\"或\"logit boosting\")\n- 概念分离在最后一层最大 → 分离是累积的\n\n### ★★★ 修正后的理论框架 (Phase 34版)\n\n```\nPhase 33误读: \"梯度方向≈因果方向\"\nPhase 34修正: \n\n1. 模型是线性/近似线性算子 (Qwen3/GLM4):\n   h_{l+k} ≈ J_{l+k-1} · J_{l+k-2} · ... · J_l · h_l + c\n   - Jacobian近似input-independent → 扰动传播跨概念一致\n   - 但Jacobian不是恒等变换 → 方向被旋转\n\n2. Jacobian链是\"旋转放大器\":\n   - ||J·δ|| > ||δ|| (放大) 但 cos(J·δ, δ) → 0 (旋转)\n   - 没有方向能贯穿全模型 — 所有方向都被混合\n\n3. 因果路径是: h → Jacobian链 → h_{L-1} → W_U → logit\n   - W_U定义\"读出方式\" (哪些维度被映射到logit)\n   - Jacobian链定义\"传播方式\" (信号如何从h_l到h_{L-1})\n   - 两者共同决定因果: 改变h_l → 经Jacobian链传播 → 在W_U读出位置产生效应\n\n4. Probe方向不是因果的, 因为:\n   - Probe学的是: 哪个方向上P(edible|h)最大 (统计相关)\n   - 因果机制是: h → Jacobian链 → W_U → logit (变换+读出)\n   - probe方向不在W_U的\"有利子空间\"中 → 传播到输出时效应弱\n\n5. lm_head方向(W_U行)是因果的, 因为:\n   - W_U直接定义了logit的读出: logit = W_U · h\n   - 沿W_U行方向扰动h → 直接影响logit\n   - 即使在浅层注入, lm_head方向也能通过Jacobian链传播到输出\n\n6. \"语义\"不是方向, 而是Jacobian链对信号的变换结果:\n   - 输入\"apple\" → h_0 → Jacobian链变换 → h_L → W_U → \"edible\"\n   - 语义(=edible)是整个变换链的输出, 不是h中的某个方向\n```\n\n### ★★★ 硬伤与关键问题\n\n1. **probe R²≈1.000太完美 → 数据集可能有完美线性可分性**\n   - 70个概念, 3个属性(edible/animacy/size), 只有3类(fruit/animal/object)\n   - 线性可分太容易 → probe R²不能说明表示质量\n   - 需要: 更多属性、更细粒度的分类、连续属性\n\n2. **DS7B的非线性来源不明**\n   - DS7B中间层CV>0.3 → Jacobian input-dependent\n   - 可能是8bit量化的artifact, 也可能是模型架构差异\n   - 需要: 对比8bit vs 16bit的DS7B\n\n3. **lm_head方向的\"强效应\"可能是trivial的**\n   - W_U直接连接h和logit → 沿W_U方向扰动当然直接影响logit\n   - 这不是\"发现了因果机制\", 只是确认了模型的基本计算图\n   - 真正的问题是: Jacobian链如何将浅层的信号传播到W_U的读取位置?\n\n4. **amplification>1意味着模型不稳定?**\n   - 扰动被放大4-25倍 → 误差也会被放大\n   - 但模型训练收敛了 → 可能在数据分布上, Jacobian的放大被其他机制平衡\n\n5. **只用了3个测试概念 → 泛化性不足**\n   - apple/dog/hammer不能代表所有语义类别\n   - 需要更多概念, 特别是边界案例(如salmon: edible+animate)\n\n### 第一性原理分析: 从\"方向\"到\"算子\"的范式转换\n\n**认知层级更新**:\n\n```\nL0: 语义是\"方向\" ← 已证伪 (Phase 26-30)\nL1: 语义是\"子空间\" ← 已修正 (Phase 31, 只是切空间)\nL2: 语义是\"非线性函数g(h)\" ← 部分证伪 (Phase 33, 至少部分是线性+零空间)\nL3: 语义是\"分布式因果结构\" ← Phase 33, 但误读了梯度\nL4: 语义是\"Jacobian链的变换结果\" ← Phase 34新认知\n```\n\n**L4的关键洞察**:\n\n1. **模型 = 线性算子链(近似) + 最终读出(W_U)**\n   - 在Qwen3/GLM4中: h → J_0 → J_1 → ... → J_{L-1} → W_U → logit\n   - Jacobian近似常数(input-independent) → 模型近似线性\n   - 但每个J_l都是\"旋转放大器\": 放大信号, 旋转方向\n\n2. **不存在贯穿全模型的\"语义方向\"**\n   - 任何方向经过几层Jacobian后被旋转(cos→0.1)\n   - → \"edible方向\"只在局部有意义(1-2层范围)\n   - → 全局因果只能通过完整的Jacobian链理解\n\n3. **W_U(读出)是因果路径的终点**\n   - lm_head方向对logit的效应远强于probe方向\n   - → \"因果\"由读出方式(W_U)和传播方式(Jacobian链)共同决定\n   - → 不是表示中的\"内在属性\", 而是表示-读出的关系\n\n4. **DS7B的中间层非线性 → 不同的计算模式**\n   - 不是所有模型都是线性算子\n   - DS7B(R1-Distill)可能在中间层做更复杂的非线性变换\n   - → 需要逐模型分析, 不能一概而论\n\n**关键数学问题更新**:\n\nPhase 33问: \"probe方向是否在A的零空间中?\"\nPhase 34答: 不需要零空间解释。更精确的说法是:\n- Probe方向不在W_U的\"有利子空间\"中 → 传播到输出时效应弱\n- 但Jacobian链确实保留了信号(只是旋转了方向) → probe方向的信息没有丢失,只是被混合了\n\n**下一步核心问题**:\n1. Jacobian的具体结构是什么? (奇异值谱/有效秩/旋转角度)\n2. 不同层的Jacobian如何不同? (浅层vs深层的放大/旋转特性)\n3. Jacobian链的\"累积旋转\"是否有规律? (类似随机游走还是确定性旋转)\n4. 能否从Jacobian链推导出\"因果归因\"? (哪个层对哪个属性最关键)\n\n[Phase 34关键验证时间标记: 2026年05月04日23时32分]\n\n"}

---

## Phase 34 总结

### 批评分析：5条全部正确

| 批评 | 评判 | 核心修正 |
|------|------|---------|
| gradient≠因果方向 | ✔ | gradient只是局部决策边界方向，input-dependent |
| probe负效应≠无用 | ✔ | probe捕捉E[y\|h](相关性)，gradient捕捉∂logit/∂h(变化率)，两者本质不同 |
| 病态矩阵解释过度 | ✔ | 高cond在高维是常态，零空间不是唯一解释 |
| 残差≠非线性语义 | ✔ | 残差可能含未拟合线性结构/噪声 |
| 内在维度≠流形 | ✔ | 可能是clustered cloud/mixture of subspaces |

### Phase 34 三大发现

**1. 模型是近似线性算子(Qwen3/GLM4)**
- 扰动传播跨概念高度一致(CV<0.15) → Jacobian近似input-independent
- DS7B中间层例外(CV>0.3) → 非线性更强

**2. 所有方向在Jacobian链中被快速旋转**
- cos(original, delta)从0.9降到0.1 → 不存在贯穿全模型的"语义方向"
- 扰动被放大(amp=4-25x)但方向被旋转 → 模型是"旋转放大器"

**3. lm_head方向是真正的因果管道**
- W_U方向对logit效应远超probe方向(5-9倍)
- 因果=Jacobian链传播 + W_U读出，不是表示中的"内在方向"

### 最严格的审视

**硬伤**:
1. **probe R²≈1.000太完美** → 70概念3属性太容易线性可分，不能说明问题
2. **lm_head"发现"可能是trivial的** → W_U直接连接h和logit，当然沿它走有效
3. **3个测试概念泛化不足** → 需要边界案例(如salmon: edible+animate)
4. **DS7B的非线性可能是8bit量化artifact**

### 突破瓶颈的第一性原理

核心洞察：**语义不是h中的方向，而是Jacobian链对信号的变换结果**。模型 = 旋转放大器链 + 读出矩阵(W_U)。

**下一阶段大任务**：从"Jacobian链的宏观行为"深入到"Jacobian的微观结构"——

1. **逐层Jacobian奇异值谱**：每层的J_l有多少有效维度？哪些方向被放大/抑制？
2. **Jacobian链的旋转累积规律**：是随机游走还是确定性旋转？能否预测？
3. **因果归因图**：哪个层对哪个属性最关键？能否从Jacobian链推导出层间的"因果贡献"？
4. **跨模型Jacobian结构比较**：Qwen3/GLM4的线性Jacobian vs DS7B的非线性Jacobian，结构差异在哪？

核心问题：你现在的三大硬伤

下面是关键——这些如果不修，你后面所有“理论升级”都会偏。

❌ 硬伤1：你把“CV小”当成“Jacobian是常数”

这是一个很危险的跳跃。

你现在的逻辑：

CV小 → 扰动传播一致 → Jacobian input-independent → 模型近似线性

问题是：

👉 CV小只说明：

不同输入在你采样的方向上响应相似

但不等价于：

Jacobian是常数矩阵

举个更直观的反例

一个函数：

f(x)=ReLU(Ax)

在：

同一个激活模式区域内
→ Jacobian 是常数

但：

跨区域 → 完全不同

👉 如果你的数据刚好落在同一个activation region

你会误以为：

“模型是线性的”

但实际上只是：

“你没走出那块局部”

✔ 正确表述应该是

你最多能说：

在当前数据分布 + 当前扰动尺度下
模型表现为局部近似线性

而不是：

模型是线性算子链

❌ 硬伤2：把“方向旋转”解释为“没有语义方向”

你现在说：

所有方向都会被旋转 → 不存在语义方向

这个结论是过度解读。

关键问题

你测的是：

cos(δ
l
	

,δ
L
	

)

但语义可能是：

g(h)

👉 如果：

表示在旋转
但函数 g 同时在“补偿”

那么：

👉 语义可以保持稳定，但方向在变

类比（非常重要）

想象：

一个向量在旋转
但分类器也在同步旋转

那么：

👉 分类结果不变
👉 但方向完全不同

✔ 所以正确结论是：

“没有跨层不变的线性方向”

❌ 而不是：

“没有语义结构”

❌ 硬伤3：lm_head方向“强”是trivial的

你已经自己隐约意识到了，但还没彻底打掉：

为什么 lm_head 强是必然的？

因为：

logit=W
U
	

⋅h

👉 沿着 W
U
	

 的方向 perturb：

h+ϵW
U
T
	

⇒logit 增加

这是定义级别成立，不是发现。

真正有价值的问题应该是：

👉 为什么：

早期层注入的扰动
能在经过几十层后
仍然对 W
U
	

 有投影？

这才是：

Jacobian链如何“保留可读信号”

你现在的结论：

lm_head是因果路径

其实只是：

读出层是读出层（废话级结论）

三、你真正接近的“核心突破点”

你其实已经碰到了一个非常关键的结构：

⭐ Jacobian链 = 信息路由系统（而不是旋转器）

你现在看到的是：

方向被旋转
norm被放大

但更深一层是：

👉 Jacobian在做：

✔ 信息“重分配”（routing）

它不是随便旋转，而是：

把某些子空间 → 映射到可读空间
把某些子空间 → 压缩/消失
更精确的形式
J=UΣV
T
V：输入方向基
Σ：放大/压缩
U：输出方向

👉 关键不是“旋转了”，而是：

哪些方向被 保留 + 放大 + 对齐到 W_U

四、真正的瓶颈（最重要）

你现在卡住的不是实验，而是识别问题定义错了

❗ 你在找“语义在哪里”

但实际上应该问：

语义是如何被计算出来的？

两种范式对比

你当前范式：

语义 = hidden state中的结构

更可能的真实情况：

语义 = 整个计算过程的结果
      = W_U · (J链 · h0)

👉 也就是说：

语义不在某一层
不在某个方向
不在某个子空间

而是：

整个网络作为一个算子，对输入的映射结果

五、下一步建议（真正关键）

你现在最该做的不是继续“测方向”，而是：

1️⃣ 做 Jacobian SVD（必须）

哪些方向被放大？
哪些被压缩？
有效秩是多少？

信息如何流动

cos(δ, δ_L)

而是测：

projection onto span(W
U
	

)

👉 看：

哪些输入方向最终变成“可读信号”

3️⃣ 跨层“语义守恒”测试（非常关键）

测试：

g(h_l) 是否 ≈ g(h_{l+k})

👉 如果成立：

✔ 支持“函数存在”
❌ 不依赖方向

六、总结一句话（最重要）

你现在最需要修正的是这一点：

❌ “语义是方向 / 子空间 / Jacobian结构”

✔ “语义是整个网络作为算子的输出性质”
